name: Cu Hawaii
description_short: CU Hawaii is a credit union serving Hawaii residents with checking, savings, loans, and credit cards. Member-focused financial cooperative offering Kasasa rewards accounts and mortgage products.
description_long:
CU Hawaii is a member-owned credit union celebrating its 70th anniversary, positioned as a community-focused financial institution serving Hawaii residents across multiple islands including Hilo, Kona, and Keaau. The organization emphasizes its cooperative model with the tagline 'we never deal with customers. We serve members, help neighbors and support communities,' distinguishing itself as a relationship-driven alternative to traditional banks.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of personal and business financial products. Personal services include Kasasa Rewards checking accounts (with variants like Kasasa Cash, Kasasa Cash Back, and Kasasa Tunes), member savings accounts, certificates of deposit, money market accounts, and Individual Retirement Accounts. They provide digital banking through online and mobile platforms with features like mobile deposit, bill pay, and telephone banking. Their lending products span personal loans, auto loans, first mortgages, home equity lines of credit, and credit cards, with current promotional rates advertised (5.50% APR intro rates on HELOCs, new auto loan offers). Business members can access business checking, merchant services, small business loans, and business credit cards.

What distinguishes CU Hawaii is their proprietary rewards structure through Kasasa partnerships, offering accounts with APY rates highlighted at 5.00% on rewards savings accounts. They provide specialized programs including a SmartScore credit builder loan program for members building credit, and community-focused initiatives like the Hawaii County Emergency Rental Assistance Program. The institution maintains physical branch locations across Hawaii and offers accessibility features on their website through AudioEye technology.

As a credit union, CU Hawaii operates with member-centric principles typical of credit unions but requires membership to access services. While they offer competitive rates and rewards programs, their geographic limitation to Hawaii restricts accessibility for mainland consumers. The website emphasizes 70th anniversary promotions and member appreciation initiatives, suggesting strong community integration but potentially limited digital-first convenience compared to larger national institutions. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Kasasa Rewards Checking (multiple variants: Kasasa Cash, Kasasa Cash Back, Kasasa Tunes)
First Mortgages and Other Home Loans
Credit Cards (CU Hawaii Mastercard with MyCard Rewards)
Online Bill Pay and Telephone Banking
pros: Kasasa Rewards checking accounts offer cash back and rewards with no minimum balance requirements
Rewards Savings Account advertised at 5.00% APY, competitive rates for savings products
Comprehensive digital banking including mobile deposit, bill pay, and online account management
SmartScore credit builder loan program specifically designed for members building credit
Home Equity Line of Credit with promotional intro rates as low as 5.50% APR for 3 years
Member-owned cooperative model with community focus and local branch presence across multiple Hawaiian islands
Diverse lending products including personal loans, auto loans, mortgages, and business loans under one institution
cons: Membership required to access services; eligibility criteria not clearly stated on homepage
Geographic limitation to Hawaii residents only, excluding mainland consumers
Limited information provided on the website about membership fees, account minimums for non-Kasasa accounts, or specific APR ranges for loans
No prominently displayed customer service ratings or member testimonials on the main pages reviewed
Small institution with fewer branches than national banks, potentially limiting convenience for some members
best_for: Hawaii residents seeking a community-focused alternative to large national banks with competitive rewards checking
Members building credit who want structured credit builder loan programs combined with traditional banking services
Small business owners in Hawaii needing integrated business banking, merchant services, and commercial lending
Savers interested in high-yield savings accounts and certificate products with personalized member service
diagnosis: CU Hawaii is best for Hawaii residents seeking membership in a community-focused credit union with competitive Kasasa rewards checking, high-yield savings, and a full range of personal and business lending products. The main caveat is membership eligibility requirements and geographic limitation to Hawaii, making it inaccessible to mainland consumers and non-qualifying Hawaii residents.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
